content.cn_text: 你将听到两个学生和他们的老师讨论一个计划好的慈善活动。在播放录音之前，你有一些时间阅读问题21-25。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: You will hear a discussion between two students and their teacher on a planned charity event. First you have some time to look at questions 21-25. [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答问题21-25。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21-25.

content.cn_text: 老师:那么，你的年度慈善活动计划有进展了吗?我想，重要的事情先说，你决定今年要资助什么慈善机构了吗?
content.en_text: Teacher: So, are you making any progress with your plans for our annual charity event? I guess, first things first, have you decided what charity it will be in aid of this year?

content.cn_text: 马克:我们在考虑帮助非洲儿童组织，先生。
content.en_text: Mark: We were thinking about Help the Children of Africa sir.

content.cn_text: 劳拉:嗯，那是马克的主意，先生，但我自己更喜欢当地的慈善机构，叫做“送餐上门”。
content.en_text: Laura: Well, that's Mark's idea sir, but I myself prefer a local charity called the Meals on Wheels.

content.cn_text: 老师:在这一点上我同意劳拉的看法，马克。毕竟，我们应该回馈当地社区，尽管帮助非洲儿童是一项非常有价值的事业，但这有点超出了我们的职权范围。
content.en_text: Teacher: I'd have to agree with Laura on this one, Mark. After all, we're supposed to be giving back to the local community and, although helping African children is a very worthy cause, it's a little outside our remit.

content.cn_text: 马克:我想这就解决了!
content.en_text: Mark: That settles that I guess!

content.cn_text: 老师:从受益人的问题开始，你已经决定是什么类型的事件了吗?
content.en_text: Teacher: Moving on from the beneficiary question, have you made a decision on what type of event it will be?

content.cn_text: 劳拉:是的，我们今年打算做点不一样的事情:我们把这个活动叫做“气球马拉松”。基本上，我们要向所有的学生出售气球。
content.en_text: Laura: Yes, we plan on doing something a little different this year: we're calling the event'Balloonathon'. Basically, we're going to offer balloons for sale to all the students.

content.cn_text: 老师:气球?我不明白你想说什么。
content.en_text: Teacher: Balloons? I don't see where you are going with this.

content.cn_text: 他们为什么要买气球?
content.en_text: Why would they want to buy a balloon?

content.cn_text: 马克:嗯，事情是这样的:我们实际上不会给他们气球。相反，我们会把他们的名字和一个特殊的电话号码写在上面，然后我们把所有的气球都放飞到空中。当气球落地时，如果有人找到一个并按下特殊的号码，那么他和买气球的学生都将赢得一张礼券。
content.en_text: Mark: Well, here's the thing: we don't actually give them the balloon. Instead, we'll write their name on it along with a special phone number and then we'll release all the balloons into the air. When they fall to the ground, if a person finds one and rings the special number, then both he and the student who've bought the balloon will win a gift voucher.

content.cn_text: 老师:听起来是个好主意;经过深思熟虑。这次气球马拉松很有新意，你不觉得吗?
content.en_text: Teacher: That sounds like an excellent idea guys; well thought out. This Balloonathon has a real novelty value attached to it don't you think?

content.cn_text: 劳拉:正是我们所说的，先生。
content.en_text: Laura: Exactly what we said, sir.

content.cn_text: 马克:唯一的缺点是气球里的气体很贵。
content.en_text: Mark: The only drawback is that the gas you put in the balloons is rather expensive.

content.cn_text: 老师:多少钱?
content.en_text: Teacher: How much?

content.cn_text: 马克:大约20英镑一罐，我们大概需要10罐。
content.en_text: Mark: About £20 per canister, and we'll need about ten.

content.cn_text: 老师:你打算吹多少个气球?
content.en_text: Teacher: And how many balloons are you planning to blow up?

content.cn_text: 马克:嗯，学校里有1000多名学生，所以即使三分之一的学生买一个，我们也需要大约350个气球。我们决定订500个，这样就不会用完。好消息是，如果我们不使用这些气罐，我们可以把它们退回去，而且气球也不贵，所以我们花了很多钱却没有得到好的回报的风险并不大。
content.en_text: Mark: Well, there are over 1,000 students in the school, so if even one third of the students buy one, we'd need about 350 balloons. We've decided to order 500 so we don't run out. The good thing is we can return the canisters of gas if we don't use them and the balloons aren't expensive so there's no real risk of us spending a lot of money without getting a good return.

content.cn_text: 老师:你们俩真是把这个问题想清楚了。给我留下了深刻的印象!
content.en_text: Teacher: You two have really thought this one out. I'm impressed!

content.cn_text: 旁白:在你听剩下的讨论之前，你有些时间看看问题26-30。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: Narrator: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 26-30. [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 现在听录音，回答问题26-30。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: 劳拉:谢谢你，先生。
content.en_text: Laura: Thank you, sir.

content.cn_text: 老师:那你觉得我们能筹到多少钱?
content.en_text: Teacher: So how much money do you think we can raise?

content.cn_text: 劳拉:嗯，每个气球大约100便士，当它装满汽油时，我们大约要花50便士。我们估计，如果我们以1.5英镑的价格出售我们的气球，我们卖掉所有500个气球，我们最终将每个气球赚取1英镑的利润，所以总共是27500英镑。
content.en_text: Laura: Well, each balloon costs about lp, and when it's filled with gas it's going to cost us about 50 pence. We reckon that if we sell our balloons at a price of £ 1.50, and we sell all 500 of them, we'll end up making a profit of £1 per balloon, so that's £  500 in total.

content.cn_text: 老师:太棒了!
content.en_text: Teacher: That's fantastic!

content.cn_text: 马克:还有更好的，先生;我们已经为我们的活动找到了一个赞助商，他将给我们1000英镑。
content.en_text: Mark: And it gets better sir; we've secured a sponsor for our event who's going to give us £1,000.

content.cn_text: 老师:你是怎么找到担保人的?
content.en_text: Teacher: How did you find a sponsor?

content.cn_text: 马克:我们接洽购买气球的气球公司问我们是否有兴趣让他们也赞助我们。
content.en_text: Mark: The balloon company we approached about buying the balloons asked us if we'd be interested in letting them sponsor us too.

content.cn_text: 老师:这对他们有什么好处?
content.en_text: Teacher: What's in it for them?

content.cn_text: 马克:他们打算在每个气球上印上他们的标志。
content.en_text: Mark: They're going to print their logo on every balloon.

content.cn_text: 老师:我认为你做得很好。
content.en_text: Teacher: I think you've done a good deal there.

content.cn_text: 劳拉:谢谢你，先生。那么我们确认订单得到你的批准了吗?
content.en_text: Laura: Thank you sir. So do we have your approval to confirm our order?

content.cn_text: 老师:当然，但是你知道，我认为如果我们下定决心的话，我们可以卖更多的气球，所以为什么不订购双倍的数量呢?1000个而不是500个?
content.en_text: Teacher: Absolutely, but you know I think we can sell more balloons if we set our minds to it, so why not order double the amount; 1,000 instead of 500?

content.cn_text: 劳拉:那我们需要十多罐汽油。
content.en_text: Laura: We're gonna need more than ten canisters of gas then.

content.cn_text: 老师:大概是两倍吧。
content.en_text: Teacher: Double the amount presumably.

content.cn_text: 劳拉:正确。
content.en_text: Laura: Correct.

content.cn_text: 老师:好的，我们开始吧。让我们把今年的慈善活动办得最成功。
content.en_text: Teacher: Okay, let's go for it. Let's make this year's charity event our most successful ever.

content.cn_text: 旁白:这是第三部分的结尾。你有半分钟的时间检查答案。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: Narrator: That's the end of Section 3. You have half a minute to check your answers. [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 现在转到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to Section 4.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
The aim of the project is to raise money to support a $15390$ specifically rather than one that operates abroad.
In this year's charity event, students will be offered the chance to buy $15391$
In order to win a prize, the finder will have to ring the $15392$
The fact that gas $15393$ cost £20 each is the only drawback.
The students think it is best to put in an order of $15394$ to make sure that they don't run out of them.

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解对话中的最终决定。老师赞同了Laura的观点，选择支持本地慈善机构而不是国际性的。文中提到"we're supposed to be giving back to the local community"，这明确指出了他们的目标是支持本地社区。虽然Mark最初提出了帮助非洲儿童的想法，但老师认为这超出了他们的职责范围。因此，"local charity"是正确答案，因为它直接对应了题目中要求的"支持特定的慈善机构"，并且强调了"本地"而非"国外"的概念。记住，在听力中，往往最后达成的共识或者权威人士（如这里的老师）的意见才是最终答案。

这道题的答案是"balloons"，因为文中明确提到了要向学生出售气球。你需要注意听到"offer"和"for sale"这样的关键词，它们是"buy"的同义表达。在这些词之后，你应该特别留意可能被购买的物品名称。文中Laura说"we're going to offer balloons for sale to all the students"，这里的"balloons"就是学生可以购买的物品。记住，在听力考试中，答案通常按顺序出现，所以在听力开始部分要特别集中注意力。另外，不要被开头多次提到的"event"或"charity event"误导，题目明确要求填写学生可以购买的东西，而不是活动本身。

这道题的正确答案是"special number"。因为题目要求填写"为了赢得奖品，找到气球的人需要做什么"。在原文中，我们可以清楚地看到，为了赢得礼品券（即奖品），找到气球的人需要拨打"special number"（特殊号码）。这里的"special number"正是气球上写的那个特殊电话号码。注意，虽然"gift voucher"也出现在同一句话中，但它是奖品本身，而不是为了赢得奖品需要做的事情。所以，正确答案应该是"special number"，而不是"gift voucher"。这道题考察了你对条件句结构的理解，以及准确提取关键信息的能力。记住，在做这类题目时，要仔细分析句子结构，找出表示条件或动作的部分，而不是结果或奖励的部分。

这道题的关键在于理解问题的重点和听力材料中的细节。题目询问的是"cost £20 each"的物品，而不是气体本身。在听力材料中，Mark提到："About £20 per canister, and we'll need about ten." 这里的"canister"（气罐）才是每个花费20英镑的物品。虽然气体很贵，但实际上是装气体的容器（canister）标价为20英镑。这要求你能够仔细分辨听力中的细节，并准确理解"per"这个词的用法。此外，"drawback"（缺点）这个词也是一个重要的信号，它提示我们接下来会谈到成本或困难，帮助我们定位到正确的信息。因此，正确答案是"canisters"而不是"gas"或其他相关词汇。

这道题的关键在于准确捕捉学生最终决定订购的气球数量。文中提到了几个不同的数字，但我们需要找到最终的决定。Mark说"We've decided to order 500 so we don't run out"，这句话明确表示他们决定订购500个气球，原因是为了不会用完。这个数量比之前提到的350个要多，显示了他们的谨慎态度。虽然文中提到如果三分之一的学生购买气球就需要350个，但实际订购的数量更多，以确保充足供应。所以，正确答案是"500 balloons"。记住，在听力题中，要特别注意表示最终决定或结论的词语，如"decided"、"in the end"等，这些往往指向最终答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-27
Complete the teachers notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer
Notes on costs and potential profit:
groups.collect:
Notes on costs and potential profit:
Unit cost: £0.50
Unit selling price: £ $15395$
Estimated total profit: £ $15396$

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是1.50英镑，因为原文中明确提到了"sell our balloons at a price of £1.50"。这里需要注意的是，题目要求的是单位售价（Unit selling price），而不是成本或利润。文中提到的1英镑是每个气球的利润，不是售价。作为学生，你要特别注意区分这些相关但不同的概念。在听力考试中，当遇到涉及数字的问题时，要格外留意与问题相关的具体数字信息，不要被其他数字干扰。这道题考察了你对细节信息的准确把握能力，以及对基本商业概念的理解。

这道题的关键在于准确理解对话中的财务计算过程。题目要求的是"Estimated total profit"（预计总利润），而原文中Laura明确解释了这个计算过程。她说如果他们以1.50英镑的价格售出所有500个气球，每个气球的利润将是1英镑，总利润就是500英镑。这里的计算逻辑是：500（气球数量）× 1英镑（每个气球的利润）= 500英镑（总利润）。虽然对话中出现了其他数字（如赞助商提供的1,000英镑），但这些并不是题目所问的总利润。因此，正确答案是500英镑。做这道题时，你需要注意听与"profit"和"total"相关的信息，并理解上下文中的具体计算过程。

groups.desc:
Questions 28-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: How did the students find an event sponsor?

options.title: A
options.content: They asked a company to sponsor the event.

options.title: B
options.content: They spent £1,000 promoting the event to attract sponsors.

options.title: C
options.content: They were approached by a company interested in being a sponsor.

items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为原文中明确提到是气球公司主动询问学生是否有兴趣让他们成为赞助商。这表明是公司主动提出赞助，而不是学生寻找或请求赞助。这与选项C "They were approached by a company interested in being a sponsor" 完全吻合，说明是一家对成为赞助商感兴趣的公司主动接洽了学生。

items.title: What does the sponsor want in return?

options.title: A
options.content: £1,000 of the profits and their logo on every balloon

options.title: B
options.content: to print their logo on the products they sell

options.title: C
options.content: approval from the teacher that it is okay to go ahead with the sponsorship

items.analysis: 文中明确提到赞助商要在每个气球上印上他们的标志，这是赞助商作为回报所要求的。这直接对应了选项B中"to print their logo on the products they sell"的描述。气球就是他们销售的产品，而印上标志正是赞助商的要求。因此，B选项最准确地反映了赞助商的要求。

items.title: How many containers of gas are they going to need if they do as the teacher suggests at the end of the conversation?
items.analysis: 文中最初提到需要10个气罐，但在对话结尾处，老师建议将气球数量增加一倍（从500增加到1000）。Laura随后确认这意味着他们需要增加气罐的数量，老师回应说："Double the amount presumably"（大概是双倍的数量），Laura确认这是正确的。因此，如果最初需要10个气罐，那么将数量翻倍后，他们将需要20个气罐，所以正确答案是C（20）。
